author: Ricky
msg:
It’s that manage.unitynode website limitation 
Once we copy response from developers option, we only get last 1000 entries

So you put in any app/ scrapper, it would overwrite and give you data for last 1000 rows only

author: Ricky
msg: To make a historical data, we may need to built it ourselves, by having different 1000 entries and combining them after few days

author: Paul
msg:
Quick question for the farmers;

I’m trying to generate a steady monthly income from my licenses. I tried the marketplace but only a few of my licenses got leased.

Now I’m considering setting up a small phone farm at home, but I’ve heard farms larger than ~20 phones may become inefficient.

For those running farms:
What size farm works best, and what kind of monthly income range are you seeing?

Any insight would really help before I move forward.

author: Marco
msg: I’m in the US and have multiple options at my house, but as most are doing in this scenario I purchased routers that accept SIM cards and purchased data only sims for 4g/5g internet access. These were added to my fiber optic and broadband isps.

author: EdgyBruh
msg: Either that or multiple ISPs

author: Paul
msg: Okay thanks, where can I learn how to set up multiple ISPs in the US?

author: Paul
msg: If I only have one Wifi connection (Xfinity) but create multiple IPs or networks on my router, how many phones can realistically run on it? How many devices have you successfully connected under one internet line?

author: Marco
msg:
I think people have connected ~100, but no one will guarantee it’s most efficient for rewards or for the real world utility of the network. They want diversity to be an organic characteristic of the edge network Unity is creating, so clustered devices aren’t encouraged. Obviously there’s always gray area when considering what’s best for you and what’s best for the project you are a part of.

Just a week or two ago if you exceeded 20 devices your rewards dropped sharply. Today it seems you can get $0.20-$0.30 per device up to 40 or more. Tomorrow? Who knows.

author: Marco
msg: Tmobile backup internet has been a popular choice. I have 2 of those lines at $20 each

msg: [Photo] There is a language setting if you select the device

author: Danny | World Market | ENO UK

author: Danny | World Market | ENO UK
